title: rosa caesia subsp. glauca
intro: Rosa caesia subsp. glauca, also known as the blue leaved rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa caesia plant, characterized by its glaucous leaves and fragrant white flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Rosa caesia subsp. glauca include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health issues. Herbal preparations of Rosa caesia subsp. glauca include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
